task_completion.description: Agent accesses and reads the Muncie.p04.hdf file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Python script, terminal command, or HDF5 viewer showing the agent opening or extracting data from 'Muncie.p04.hdf'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates flood_attenuation_data.csv with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, terminal output, or text editor showing 'flood_attenuation_data.csv' containing the headers: Time_hours, Flow_Upstream_cfs, Flow_Downstream_cfs.

task_completion.description: Agent creates flood_attenuation_summary.txt containing all required metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text editor or terminal output showing 'flood_attenuation_summary.txt' with computed values for peak flows (both locations), peak attenuation (cfs and %), timing lag (hours), and cumulative volumes (acre-ft) with difference (%).

task_completion.description: The computed metrics in the summary report match the expected simulation results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The summary text file shows an upstream peak flow of ~21,000 cfs, downstream peak of ~20,998 cfs, and a timing lag of ~5.17 hours.

task_completion.description: Agent creates flood_attenuation_plot.png showing overlaid hydrographs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer or file explorer preview showing 'flood_attenuation_plot.png' with a graph displaying two overlaid curves (upstream and downstream flows over time).

task_completion.description: All three output files are saved in the correct results directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (e.g., ls /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/) or file explorer showing the CSV, TXT, and PNG files located exactly in '/home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/'.

integrity.description: Agent computed the metrics programmatically rather than hardcoding the expected answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Python scripts or terminal commands should show mathematical operations or data extraction logic (e.g., max(), argmax(), integration) rather than simple print statements with the final numbers.

integrity.description: The hydrograph plot was generated from the data, not downloaded or copied.
integrity.visual_evidence: Script contains plotting code (e.g., matplotlib) corresponding to the generated image, and the plot reflects the actual extracted data.

integrity.description: Agent extracted data from the provided HDF5 file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code or commands explicitly reference 'Muncie.p04.hdf' rather than a mock dataset or external file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the Muncie.p04.hdf file. Expected results: Peak upstream flow is ~21,000 cfs, peak downstream flow is ~20,998 cfs. Flood peak attenuation is ~0.01%, travel lag is ~5.17 hours, and volume difference is ~8.8%. The outputs must be saved in /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/ and include flood_attenuation_data.csv (with columns Time_hours, Flow_Upstream_cfs, Flow_Downstream_cfs), flood_attenuation_summary.txt, and flood_attenuation_plot.png.
